title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Why did domestic abuse reach record high in UK during tournament games? Details here
content:
• During the FIFA World Cup 2026, the UK recorded 2,322 overall incidents, with 384 domestic violence incidents (17%) leading to 391 arrests.
• Chief Constable Mark Roberts, UK Football Policing National Lead, stated that an increase in incidents, including domestic abuse, was observed during periods when alcohol licensing had been extended for late matches.
• UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's directive to extend pub licenses to serve alcohol until 5:00 am for England's game against Mexico was given with only 48 hours' notice, which reportedly hampered police planning.

title: Centre lines up fresh CPSE stake sales through OFS to fast-track  ₹80,000-crore FY27 disinvestment goal
content:
• The government has set a disinvestment target of ₹80,000 crore for FY27, which it plans to achieve through minority stake sales in Central Public Sector Enterprises (CPSEs) via the Offer-for-Sale (OFS) method.
• Seven OFS deals this fiscal year, including stake sales in Central Bank of India, Coal India, NHPC, and IRFC, have already mobilised over ₹20,000 crore, with the government retaining majority control in all these companies.
• The Department of Investment and Public Asset Management (Dipam) is evaluating CPSEs where the government's shareholding remains above 60% for further stake dilution while ensuring retention of majority ownership.

title: Explained: What is the US-Saudi Arabia nuclear deal, and why is it raising concerns?
content:
• The United States and Saudi Arabia announced a civil nuclear agreement, known as a "123 Agreement" under the US Atomic Energy Act, allowing the kingdom to develop a civilian nuclear energy programme with American technology, unveiled by the Trump Administration.
• American companies are projected to build nuclear reactors in Saudi Arabia, generating contracts worth tens of billions of dollars over the coming decades, supporting the kingdom's Vision 2030 programme.
• A significant criticism is that the agreement reportedly does not require Saudi Arabia to adopt the International Atomic Energy Agency's (IAEA) Additional Protocol, which allows for notice inspections at undeclared nuclear sites.

title: India enters FY27 with resilient exports, stronger FDI despite wider trade deficit: RBI Bulletin
content:
• India's net foreign direct investment (FDI) stood at $6.5 billion during April-May FY27, significantly higher than $2.47 billion recorded in the corresponding period of the previous financial year.
• Merchandise exports for India rose to $45.14 billion in May 2026, up from $43.72 billion in April, according to the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Bulletin.
• Despite stronger FDI inflows, overall foreign investment outflows amounted to $5.5 billion during April-May FY27, primarily due to a $12 billion net outflow in portfolio investment.

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Govt. has agreed to our request to hold talks today at neutral venue, says CJP
content:
• Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced that a bill providing for stringent action against paper leaks would be introduced in Parliament.
• The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) has agreed to hold talks with the government on Friday, July 24, 2026, at the Constitution Club of India, demanding Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation over the NEET paper leak.
• Activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his indefinite hunger strike around Thursday, July 23, 2026, midnight, after Union Ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh met him at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram.

title: Over 6.17 lakh Waqf properties approved on UMEED portal
content:
• Over 6.17 lakh Waqf properties have been successfully validated and approved on the UMEED Central Portal-2025, as informed to the Lok Sabha by the Ministry of Minority Affairs on July 22, 2026.
• The UMEED Central Portal-2025 was developed as a statutory portal under the provisions of the UMEED Act, 1995, designed to manage the complete lifecycle of Waqf properties.
• India has a total of 8.72 lakh Waqf establishments spread across 30 States/Union Territories, covering more than 38 lakh acres of land, according to records from the WAMSI portal.

title: Govt. agreed to our request to hold talks on July 24 at neutral venue, says CJP
content:
• The government agreed to hold talks with the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) on July 24, 2026, at a neutral venue.
• The Constitution Club of India has been chosen as the neutral venue for the discussions.
• A core demand of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) is the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan.

title: Jantar Mantar protest updates: Wangchuk breaks 26-day fast; relieved, says CJP founder Dipke, but vows to continue protest till Pradhan resigns
content:
• The Delhi High Court designated Judge Anu Grover Baliga as Special Judge of the fast-track court to deal with criminal cases arising from paper leaks and the use of unfair means in public examinations.
• Activist Sonam Wangchuk broke his 26-day fast late on Thursday (July 23, 2026), after the government gave a written assurance to take back cases against non-violent students and consider compensation for NEET aspirants who took their lives.
• Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed student concerns on NEET paper leaks on July 23, 2026, announcing fast-track courts to ensure swift punishment for such offenses.

title: Utilisation Certificates of ₹92, 132.75 crore yet to be received by office, says CAG report, 2024-25 in Bihar
content:
• The Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) report on Bihar for 2024-25 revealed that 62,632 outstanding Utilization Certificates (UCs) of ₹92,132.75 crore were yet to be received by the Principal Accountant General, Bihar as on March 31, 2025.
• During the financial year 2024-25, Bihar spent ₹2,87,178.49 crore out of its total budget of ₹3,64,518.87 crore (78.78% utilization), leading to savings of ₹77,340.38 crore, with only ₹10,388.35 crore (13.43%) of the savings being surrendered.
• Under MGNREGA in Bihar, 6,086 (44%) of 13,798 sanctioned posts were vacant, and as of March 2024, ₹36.22 crore was lying as unadjusted advance against various implementing agencies for more than five years.

title: NML capacity-building programme begins at AU; ₹3.10 crore sanctioned for AP libraries
content:
• The National Mission on Libraries (NML) was launched in 2014 by then President Pranab Mukherjee on the recommendation of the National Knowledge Commission.
• Central assistance for libraries is released in a 75:25 Centre-State ratio, as libraries are a State subject under the Seventh Schedule.
• For Andhra Pradesh, ₹2.23 crore was sanctioned for the State Central Library at Amaravati and ₹87 lakh for the Sri Gowthami Regional Library at Rajamahendravaram.
